title: Dortmanna diversifolia
taxonomy: Dortmanna diversifolia is classified in the family Haloragaceae, which is a family of aquatic plants commonly known as the water-doe family or the water-dragon family. The genus Dortmanna is a small group of flowering plants that are native to Europe and Asia. Dortmanna diversifolia is a species within this genus and is also known as the European water-dragon. The plant is a perennial herb that grows in wet environments, including rivers, streams, and ponds. It is a relatively rare and local species, primarily found in the temperate regions of Europe and Asia.
